title: ENPIRE: Agentic Robot Policy Self-Improvement in the Real World
summary: ENPIRE is a framework enabling coding agents to autonomously improve robotic manipulation policies through a repeatable feedback loop. It integrates four core modules: Environment (automatic reset and verification), Policy Improvement (refinement algorithms), Rollout (physical robot evaluation), and Evolution (agent analysis and code optimization). Frontier coding agents achieved a 99% success rate on challenging dexterous manipulation tasks including PushT, pin insertion, and zip-tie cutting. The framework supports heuristic learning, behavior cloning, and offline/online RL, and scales across robot fleets.
